topic: A person finds out their biography was written without their knowledge
raw_output:
[story 1]

The crisp, cream-colored paper felt alien beneath my fingertips. I traced the elegant, flowing script, the words detailing my life—my triumphs, my supposed sorrows, the very trajectory of my existence—and a cold knot tightened in my stomach. Every sentence was a carefully constructed façade, a narrative I hadn't authored. I reread the final paragraph, the one describing my 'ascension,' and a tremor ran through my hand, causing the ink to smudge slightly. My breath hitched. I pushed the document away, the sound of the paper rustling loud in the otherwise silent study. A strange, humming tension vibrated just beneath my skin, demanding an explanation I couldn't yet locate.

[story 2]

My eyes scanned the printed pages, a frantic sweep that refused to settle on any single detail. The sheer audacity of it—that someone had mapped my soul onto paper without my consent—made my jaw clench so tightly I could taste copper. I leaned back from the desk, the leather chair groaning in protest, and ran a hand through my hair, feeling the sudden prickle across my scalp. I picked up a pen, my movements jerky, and began to cross out phrases, the graphite scratching harshly against the expensive stock. A low, insistent thrumming echoed in my ears, a persistent internal alarm signaling that the quiet reality I inhabited was suddenly fractured.

[story 3]

I stared at the biographical sketch, my gaze fixed on the discrepancy between the recorded events and the memories I held. A wave of nausea washed over me, and I had to grip the edge of the mahogany desk to keep from swaying. My pulse hammered a frantic rhythm against my ribs. "Impossible," I whispered, the sound raspy in the stillness of the room. I flipped through the sections, searching for the seam, the point where the fabrication began. My fingers moved with a desperate speed, searching for a flaw in the structure, driven by a profound sense of wrongness that settled deep in my core.
